mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable model serving. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in setup for distributed environments.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

helm repo update

```

> This adds the specific version of the vLLM repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install vLLM with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs vLLM into the specified namespace with the desired configuration.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> Installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Custom Resource for vLLM
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) that defines the configuration for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'llama'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Replace 'llama' with your desired model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: my-vllm
spec:
  model: 'llama'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM deployment and ensures that the pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n vllm

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> You should see the vLLM pods in a Running state.
> Verify the status of your custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your vLLM pod.
> Upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Helm repository may not be added correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-add the Helm repository using 'helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0'.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the model configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in your Custom Resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not be available.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory efficiency.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM pods running with the output: 'Running' under the STATUS column when executing 'kubectl get pods -n vllm'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
